scope.target: Documentation-first refactor workflow definition for .dev and .ai maintenance
scope.dependency_radius: Workflow artifacts and human-facing workflow guides only
constraints: Must preserve current workflow mode artifact locations under .dev/workflows/
Must adapt execution semantics for document work without breaking code-refactor workflows
Must define document validation in terms of completeness, ownership, and source-of-truth clarity
non_goals: Do not perform broad content cleanup across .dev and .ai in this task
Do not redesign all skills or sub-agent boundaries

inputs.architecture_target: Refactor workflow must explicitly support document completion/refactoring as a first-class staged workflow before any broad documentation maintenance work begins.
review_findings: Current staged-refactor guidance is code-centric and can mislead document-maintenance execution.
user_constraints: This work must go through refactor workflow.
If the workflow only supports code refactoring, prioritize planning a document refactor workflow first.

steps: Define how document refactor stages differ from code refactor stages.
Specify artifact expectations and validation criteria for documentation tasks.
Identify which existing workflow docs require amendment or extension.
Record deferred items that belong to later content stages rather than workflow-definition stage.
validation: Workflow docs no longer imply code is the only valid refactor target.
At least one explicit document-first staging pattern exists.
Task can be consumed by a later implementer without architectural guessing.

results.summary: Extended the refactor workflow guidance so workflow mode explicitly supports document refactor/document completion work before broader documentation maintenance stages begin.
